links.title: TRUST-SQL: Tool-Integrated Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-SQL over Unknown Schemas

primary_link.title: TRUST-SQL: Tool-Integrated Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-SQL over Unknown Schemas

papers.summary: Text-to-SQL parsing has achieved remarkable progress under the Full Schema Assumption. However, this premise fails in real-world enterprise environments where databases contain hundreds of tables with massive noisy metadata. Rather than injecting the full schema upfront, an agent must actively identify and verify only the relevant subset, giving rise to the Unknown Schema scenario we study in this work. To address this, we propose TRUST-SQL (Truthful Reasoning with Unknown Schema via Tools). We formulate the task as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process where our autonomous agent employs a structured four-phase protocol to ground reasoning in verified metadata. Crucially, this protocol provides a structural boundary for our novel Dual-Track GRPO strategy. By applying token-level masked advantages, this strategy isolates exploration rewards from execution outcomes to resolve credit assignment, yielding a 9.9% relative improvement over standard GRPO. Extensive experiments across five benchmarks demonstrate that TRUST-SQL achieves an average absolute improvement of 30.6% and 16.6% for the 4B and 8B variants respectively over their base models. Remarkably, despite operating entirely without pre-loaded metadata, our framework consistently matches or surpasses strong baselines that rely on schema prefilling.
papers.summary_zh: Text-to-SQL parsing 在 Full Schema Assumption 下取得了顯著進展。然而，在真實世界的企業環境中，資料庫包含數百個帶有大量雜訊 metadata 的表格，這個前提便失效了。代理不應預先注入完整 Schema，而必須主動識別並驗證僅相關的子集，這產生了我們在這項工作中研究的 Unknown Schema 情境。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 TRUST-SQL (Truthful Reasoning with Unknown Schema via Tools)。我們將此任務表述為一個 Partially Observable Markov Decision Process，其中我們的自主代理採用結構化的四階段協議，將推理 grounded 於已驗證的 metadata 中。至關重要的是，這個協議為我們新穎的 Dual-Track GRPO 策略提供了一個結構性邊界。透過應用 token-level masked advantages，此策略將探索獎勵與執行結果分開，以解決 credit assignment 問題，相較於標準 GRPO 產生了 9.9% 的相對改進。跨越五個 benchmarks 的廣泛實驗表明，TRUST-SQL 在其基礎模型上，對於 4B 和 8B 變體分別取得了 30.6% 和 16.6% 的平均絕對改進。值得注意的是，儘管完全在沒有預載入 metadata 的情況下運行，我們的框架仍始終匹配或超越依賴於 schema prefilling 的強大 baselines。
papers.title: TRUST-SQL: Tool-Integrated Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-SQL over Unknown Schemas
papers.title_zh: TRUST-SQL：用於未知 Schema 的 Tool-Integrated Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning Text-to-SQL

papers.summary: Public leaderboards increasingly suggest that large language models (LLMs) surpass human experts on benchmarks spanning academic knowledge, law, and programming. Yet most benchmarks are fully public, their questions widely mirrored across the internet, creating systematic risk that models were trained on the very data used to evaluate them. This paper presents three complementary experiments forming a rigorous multi-method contamination audit of six frontier LLMs: GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, DeepSeek-R

links.title: InCoder-32B: Code Foundation Model for Industrial Scenarios

primary_link.title: InCoder-32B: Code Foundation Model for Industrial Scenarios

papers.summary: Recent code large language models have achieved remarkable progress on general programming tasks. Nevertheless, their performance degrades significantly in industrial scenarios that require reasoning about hardware semantics, specialized language constructs, and strict resource constraints. To address these challenges, we introduce InCoder-32B (Industrial-Coder-32B), the first 32B-parameter code foundation model unifying code intelligence across chip design, GPU kernel optimization, embedded systems, compiler optimization, and 3D modeling. By adopting an efficient architecture, we train InCoder-32B from scratch with general code pre-training, curated industrial code annealing, mid-training that progressively extends context from 8K to 128K tokens with synthetic industrial reasoning data, and post-training with execution-grounded verification. We conduct extensive evaluation on 14 mainstream general code benchmarks and 9 industrial benchmarks spanning 4 specialized domains. Results show InCoder-32B achieves highly competitive performance on general tasks while establishing strong open-source baselines across industrial domains.
papers.summary_zh: 最近的程式碼大型語言模型在一般程式設計任務上取得了顯著進展。然而，在需要對硬體語義、專用語言結構和嚴格資源限制進行推理的工業場景中，它們的性能顯著下降。為了應對這些挑戰，我們引入了 InCoder-32B (Industrial-Coder-32B)，這是第一個 32B 參數的程式碼基礎模型，它統一了晶片設計、GPU kernel 優化、嵌入式系統、編譯器優化和 3D modeling 領域的程式碼智慧。通過採用高效的架構，我們從頭開始訓練 InCoder-32B，包括通用程式碼 pre-training、精選工業程式碼 annealing、使用合成工業推理數據將 context 從 8K 逐步擴展到 128K tokens 的 mid-training，以及帶有 execution-grounded verification 的 post-training。我們對 14 個主流通用程式碼 benchmarks 和 4 個專業領域的 9 個工業 benchmarks 進行了廣泛評估。結果顯示 InCoder-32B 在通用任務上實現了極具競爭力的性能，同時在工業領域建立了強大的開源 baselines。
papers.title: InCoder-32B: Code Foundation Model for Industrial Scenarios

links.title: Reliable Reasoning in SVG-LLMs via Multi-Task Multi-Reward Reinforcement Learning

primary_link.title: Reliable Reasoning in SVG-LLMs via Multi-Task Multi-Reward Reinforcement Learning

papers.summary: With the rapid advancement of vision-language models, an increasing number of studies have explored their potential for SVG generation tasks. Although existing approaches improve performance by constructing large-scale SVG datasets and introducing SVG-specific tokens, they still suffer from limited generalization, redundant paths in code outputs, and a lack of explicit reasoning. In this work, we present CTRL-S (Chain-of-Thought Reinforcement Learning for SVG), a unified framework that introduces a chain-of-thought mechanism to explicitly expose the model's reasoning process during SVG generation. To support this structured reasoning, we construct SVG-Sophia, a high-quality dataset containing 145K samples across SVG code refinement, Text-to-SVG, and Image-to-SVG tasks. By training the model to generate group-level structured SVG code, CTRL-S significantly improves structural coherence and visual fidelity. Furthermore, we adopt the GRPO algorithm and design a multi-reward optimization framework, incorporating DINO, image-text similarity, format, and code efficiency rewards. Through joint multi-reward optimization and multi-task training, our approach systematically enhances overall generation capabilities. Extensive experiments show that CTRL-S outperforms existing methods, achieving higher task success rates, superior SVG code quality, and exceptional visual fidelity.
papers.summary_zh: 隨著 vision-language models 的快速發展，越來越多的研究探索了它們在 SVG generation 任務中的潛力。儘管現有方法通過構建大型 SVG datasets 和引入 SVG-specific tokens 來提高性能，但它們仍然存在泛化能力有限、程式碼輸出中路徑冗餘以及缺乏明確推理的問題。在這項工作中，我們提出了 CTRL-S (Chain-of-Thought Reinforcement Learning for SVG)，這是一個統一的框架，引入了 chain-of-thought 機制，以在 SVG generation 過程中明確地揭示模型的推理過程。為了支援這種結構化推理，我們構建了 SVG-Sophia，這是一個高質量的數據集，包含 145K 個樣本，涵蓋 SVG code refinement、Text-to-SVG 和 Image-to-SVG 任務。通過訓練模型生成 group-level structured SVG code，CTRL-S 顯著提高了結構連貫性和視覺保真度。此外，我們採用了 GRPO 演算法並設計了一個 multi-reward optimization 框架，其中包含 DINO、image-text similarity、format 和 code efficiency 獎勵。通過聯合 multi-reward optimization 和 multi-task training，我們的方法系統地增強了整體生成能力。大量的實驗表明，CTRL-S 優於現有方法，實現了更高的任務成功率、卓越的 SVG code quality 和出色的視覺保真度。

radar.summary_zh: 實現 personalized intelligence 面臨一個核心困境：將用戶歷史記錄發送到 centralized large language models 會引發隱私問題，而 on-device small language models 則缺乏實現 high-quality generation 所需的推理能力。我們的 pilot study 表明，純粹的 local enhancements 仍不足以可靠地彌合這一鴻溝。因此，我們提出了 SpecSteer，這是一個 asymmetric collaborative inference 框架，它將 private on-device context 與 cloud-scale reasoning 相結合。Spe
radar.title: SpecSteer: Synergizing Local Context and Global Reasoning for Efficient Personalized Generation
radar.title_zh: SpecSteer：協同本地語境與全球推理以實現高效個性化生成

primary_link.title: CoMAI: A Collaborative Multi-Agent Framework for Robust and Equitable Interview Evaluation

This paper presents CoMAI, a general-purpose multi-agent interview framework designed for diverse assessment scenarios. In contrast to monolithic single-agent systems based on large language models (LLMs), CoMAI employs a modular task-decomposition architecture coordinated through a centralized finite-state machine. The system comprises four agents specialized in question generation, security, scoring,
radar.summary_zh: 確保 robust 且 fair 的 interview assessment 仍然是 AI-driven evaluation 中的一個關鍵挑戰。本文提出了 CoMAI，這是一個通用型 multi-agent interview 框架，專為多樣化的 assessment scenarios 設計。與基於 large language models (LLMs) 的 monolithic single-agent systems 不同，CoMAI 採用了透過 centralized finite-state machine 協調的 modular task-decomposition architecture。該系統由四個專門負責 question generation、security、scoring 的 agents 組成。

radar.summary_zh: 隨著 Large Language Models (LLMs) 從 information providers 轉變為 Internet of Things (IoT) 中的 embodied agents，它們在 reliability 和 interaction efficiency 方面面臨著重大挑戰。直接執行 LLM-generated commands 通常會導致 entity hallucinations（例如，嘗試控制不存在的設備）。同時，現有的 iterative frameworks（例如 SAGE）遭受著 Interaction Frequency Dilemma 的困擾，在 reckless execution 和 excessive user question 之間搖擺不定。

links.title: Efficient Reasoning on the Edge

primary_link.title: Efficient Reasoning on the Edge

top5.summary: Large language models (LLMs) with chain-of-thought reasoning achieve state-of-the-art performance across complex problem-solving tasks, but their verbose reasoning traces and large context requirements make them impractical for edge deployment. These challenges include high token generation costs, large KV-cache footprints, and inefficiencies when distilling reasoning capabilities into smaller models for mobile devices. Existing approaches often rely on distilling reasoning traces from larger models into smaller models, which are verbose and stylistically redundant, undesirable for on-device inference. In this work, we propose a lightweight approach to enable reasoning in small LLMs using LoRA adapters combined with supervised fine-tuning. We further introduce budget forcing via reinforcement learning on these adapters, significantly reducing response length with minimal accuracy loss. To address memory-bound decoding, we exploit parallel test-time scaling, improving accuracy at minor latency increase. Finally, we present a dynamic adapter-switching mechanism that activates reasoning only when needed and a KV-cache sharing strategy during prompt encoding, reducing time-to-first-token for on-device inference. Experiments on Qwen2.5-7B demonstrate that our method achieves efficient, accurate reasoning under strict resource constraints, making LLM reasoning practical for mobile scenarios. Videos demonstrating our solution running on mobile devices are available on our project page.
top5.summary_zh: 採用 chain-of-thought 推理的 Large language models (LLMs) 在複雜問題解決任務中取得了 state-of-the-art 表現，但其冗長的推理軌跡和龐大的上下文需求使其不適用於 edge deployment。這些挑戰包括高昂的 token 生成成本、龐大的 KV-cache 足跡，以及在將推理能力 distillation 到用於行動裝置的較小模型時的低效率。現有方法通常依賴於將來自較大模型的推理軌跡 distilling 到較小模型中，這些軌跡冗長且風格重複，對於 on-device inference 來說並不理想。在這項工作中，我們提出了一種輕量級方法，透過結合 supervised fine-tuning 的 LoRA adapters，使小型 LLMs 能夠進行推理。我們進一步透過在這些 adapters 上應用 reinforcement learning 引入 budget forcing，顯著減少了回應長度，同時保持了最小的準確性損失。為了解決 memory-bound decoding 問題，我們利用 parallel test-time scaling，在略微增加 latency 的情況下提高了準確性。最後，我們提出了一種動態 adapter-switching 機制，僅在需要時啟用推理，以及一種在 prompt encoding 期間的 KV-cache sharing 策略，減少了 on-device inference 的 time-to-first-token。在 Qwen2.5-7B 上的實驗表明，我們的方法在嚴格的資源限制下實現了高效、準確的推理，使 LLM 推理在 mobile scenarios 中變得實用。演示我們解決方案在行動裝置上運行的影片可在我們的專案頁面上獲取。
top5.title: Efficient Reasoning on the Edge
top5.title_zh: 邊緣設備上的高效推理

links.title: MolmoB0T: Large-Scale Simulation Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation

primary_link.title: MolmoB0T: Large-Scale Simulation Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation

top5.summary: A prevailing view in robot learning is that simulation alone is not enough; effective sim-to-real transfer is widely believed to require at least some real-world data collection or task-specific fine-tuning to bridge the gap between simulated and physical environments. We challenge that assumption. With sufficiently large-scale and diverse simulated synthetic training data, we show that zero-shot transfer to the real world is not only possible, but effective for both static and mobile manipulation. We introduce MolmoBot-Engine, a fully open-source pipeline for procedural data generation across robots, tasks, and diverse simulated environments in MolmoSpaces. With it, we release MolmoBot-Data, a dataset of 1.8 million expert trajectories for articulated object manipulation and pick-and-place tasks. We train three policy classes: MolmoBot, a Molmo2-based multi-frame vision-language model with a flow-matching action head; MolmoBot-Pi0, which replicates the π_0 architecture to enable direct comparison; and MolmoBot-SPOC, a lightweight policy suitable for edge deployment and amenable to RL fine-tuning. We evaluate on two robotic platforms: the Franka FR3 for tabletop manipulation tasks and the Rainbow Robotics RB-Y1 mobile manipulator for door opening, drawer manipulation, cabinet interaction, and mobile pick-and-place. Without any real-world fine-tuning, our policies achieve zero-shot transfer to unseen objects and environments. On tabletop pick-and-place, MolmoBot achieves a success rate of 79.2% in real world evaluations across 4 settings, outperforming π_{0.5} at 39.2%. Our results demonstrate that procedural environment generation combined with diverse articulated assets can produce robust manipulation policies that generalize broadly to the real world. Technical Blog: https://allenai.org/blog/molmobot-robot-manipulation
top5.summary_zh: 在機器人學習中，一個普遍的觀點是僅靠模擬是不夠的；人們普遍認為，有效的 sim-to-real transfer 需要至少一些真實世界的數據收集或針對特定任務的 fine-tuning，以彌合模擬和物理環境之間的鴻溝。我們挑戰了這一假設。透過足夠大規模且多樣化的模擬合成訓練數據，我們展示了 zero-shot transfer 到真實世界不僅是可能的，而且對於靜態和行動 manipulation 都有效。我們引入了 MolmoBot-Engine，這是一個完全開源的管道，用於在 MolmoSpaces 中跨機器人、任務和多樣化的模擬環境生成程序性數據。隨之，我們發布了 MolmoBot-Data，一個包含 180 萬條 expert trajectories 的數據集，用於 articulated object manipulation 和 pick-and-place 任務。我們訓練了三種 policy 類別：MolmoBot，一個基於 Molmo2 的 multi-frame vision-language model，帶有 flow-matching action head；MolmoBot-Pi0，它複製了 π_0 架構以實現直接比較；以及 MolmoBot-SPOC，一個適用於 edge deployment 且易於 RL fine-tuning 的輕量級 policy。我們在兩個機器人平台上進行了評估：用於 tabletop manipulation 任務的 Franka FR3 和用於開門、抽屜 manipulation、櫃子互動以及 mobile pick-and-place 的 Rainbow Robotics RB-Y1 mobile manipulator。在沒有任何真實世界 fine-tuning 的情況下，我們的 policies 實現了對未見物體和環境的 zero-shot transfer。在 tabletop pick-and-place 任務中，MolmoBot 在 4 種設定的真實世界評估中實現了 79.2% 的成功率，優於 π_{0.5} 的 39.2%。我們的結果表明，程序性環境生成結合多樣化的 articulated assets 可以產生強健的 manipulation policies，這些 policies 可以廣泛地泛化到真實世界。技術部落格：https://allenai.org/blog/molmobot-robot-manipulation
top5.title: MolmoB0T: Large-Scale Simulation Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation
top5.title_zh: MolmoB0T：大規模模擬實現零樣本操作

links.title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents

primary_link.title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents

top5.summary: Recent advances in multimodal agents have improved computer-use interaction and tool-usage, yet most existing systems remain reactive, optimizing actions in isolation without reasoning about future states or long-term goals. This limits planning coherence and prevents agents from reliably solving high-level, multi-step tasks. We introduce TraceR1, a two-stage reinforcement learning framework that explicitly trains anticipatory reasoning by forecasting short-horizon trajectories before execution. The first stage performs trajectory-level reinforcement learning with rewards that enforce global consistency across predicted action sequences. The second stage applies grounded reinforcement fine-tuning, using execution feedback from frozen tool agents to refine step-level accuracy and executability. TraceR1 is evaluated across seven benchmarks, covering online computer-use, offline computer-use benchmarks, and multimodal tool-use reasoning tasks, where it achieves substantial improvements in planning stability, execution robustness, and generalization over reactive and single-stage baselines. These results show that anticipatory trajectory reasoning is a key principle for building multimodal agents that can reason, plan, and act effectively in complex real-world environments.
top5.summary_zh: 近期 multimodal agents 的進展改善了電腦使用互動和工具使用，然而大多數現有系統仍然是 reactive 的，單獨優化動作，而沒有對未來狀態或長期目標進行推理。這限制了規劃的一致性，並阻礙了 agents 可靠地解決高階、多步驟任務。我們引入了 TraceR1，這是一個兩階段的 reinforcement learning 框架，透過在執行前預測 short-horizon trajectories，明確地訓練 anticipatory reasoning。第一階段執行 trajectory-level reinforcement learning，其 rewards 強制了預測動作序列的全局一致性。第二階段應用 grounded reinforcement fine-tuning，利用來自 frozen tool agents 的執行回饋來精煉 step-level accuracy 和 executability。TraceR1 在七個 benchmarks 上進行了評估，涵蓋了 online computer-use、offline computer-use benchmarks 和 multimodal tool-use reasoning 任務，它在規劃穩定性、執行魯棒性和泛化能力方面，相較於 reactive 和 single-stage baselines，取得了實質性的改進。這些結果表明，anticipatory trajectory reasoning 是構建能夠在複雜真實世界環境中有效推理、規劃和行動的 multimodal agents 的關鍵原則。
top5.title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents
top5.title_zh: 多模態 AI Agent 的預測性規劃

links.title: Grid-World Representations in Transformers Reflect Predictive Geometry

primary_link.title: Grid-World Representations in Transformers Reflect Predictive Geometry

top5.summary: Next-token predictors often appear to develop internal representations of the latent world and its rules. The probabilistic nature of these models suggests a deep connection between the structure of the world and the geometry of probability distributions. In order to understand this link more precisely, we use a minimal stochastic process as a controlled setting: constrained random walks on a two-dimensional lattice that must reach a fixed endpoint after a predetermined number of steps. top5.summary_zh: next-token predictors 似乎經常發展出對潛在世界及其規則的內部 representations。這些模型的概率性質表明世界結構與概率分佈的幾何形狀之間存在深層聯繫。為了更精確地理解這種聯繫，我們使用一個最小的 stochastic process 作為受控設定：在二維 lattice 上的受限 random walks，必須在預定步數後到達一個固定的終點。 Optimal (截斷)

links.title: Online Experiential Learning for Language Models

primary_link.title: Online Experiential Learning for Language Models

top5.summary: The prevailing paradigm for improving large language models relies on offline training with human annotations or simulated environments, leaving the rich experience accumulated during real-world deployment entirely unexploited. We propose Online Experiential Learning (OEL), a framework that enables language models to continuously improve from their own deployment experience. OEL operates in two stages: first, transferable experiential knowledge is extracted and accumulated from interaction trajectories collected on the user side; second, this knowledge is consolidated into model parameters via on-policy context distillation, requiring no access to the user-side environment. The two stages are iterated to form an online learning loop, where the improved model collects higher-quality trajectories that yield richer experiential knowledge for subsequent rounds. We evaluate OEL on text-based game environments across multiple model scales and both thinking and non-thinking variants. OEL achieves consistent improvements over successive iterations, enhancing both task accuracy and token efficiency while preserving out-of-distribution performance. Our analysis further shows that extracted experiential knowledge is significantly more effective than raw trajectories, and that on-policy consistency between the knowledge source and the policy model is critical for effective learning.
top5.summary_zh: 改善 Large language models 的主流範式依賴於透過人類註釋或模擬環境進行的 offline 訓練，這使得在真實世界 deployment 期間累積的豐富經驗完全未被利用。我們提出了 Online Experiential Learning (OEL)，這是一個使語言模型能夠從其自身的 deployment 經驗中持續改進的框架。OEL 分兩個階段運行：首先，從用戶端收集的 interaction trajectories 中提取和積累可轉移的 experiential knowledge；其次，透過 on-policy context distillation 將這些知識整合到 model parameters 中，無需訪問用戶端環境。這兩個階段迭代形成一個 online learning loop，其中改進後的模型收集更高品質的 trajectories，為後續輪次產生更豐富的 experiential knowledge。我們在多種 model scales 以及 thinking 和 non-thinking 變體的 text-based game environments 上評估了 OEL。OEL 在連續迭代中實現了持續改進，在提高 task accuracy 和 token efficiency 的同時保留了 out-of-distribution performance。我們的分析進一步表明，提取的 experiential knowledge 明顯比 raw trajectories 更有效，並且 knowledge source 和 policy model 之間的 on-policy consistency 對於有效學習至關重要。
top5.title: Online Experiential Learning for Language Models
top5.title_zh: 語言模型的線上經驗學習
